comment_text.sn10.9:2.4: I have translated the slightly varying texts here and at Thig 3.6 the same, except this line which is clearly different.

root_text.sn10.9:1.1: Ekaṁ samayaṁ bhagavā rājagahe viharati veḷuvane kalandakanivāpe.
root_text.sn10.9:1.2: Tena kho pana samayena sukkā bhikkhunī mahatiyā parisāya parivutā dhammaṁ deseti.
root_text.sn10.9:1.3: Atha kho sukkāya bhikkhuniyā abhippasanno yakkho rājagahe rathikāya rathikaṁ siṅghāṭakena siṅghāṭakaṁ upasaṅkamitvā tāyaṁ velāyaṁ imā gāthāyo abhāsi:

translation_text.sn10.9:1.1: At one time the Buddha was staying near Rājagaha, in the Bamboo Grove, the squirrels’ feeding ground.
translation_text.sn10.9:1.2: Now, at that time the nun Sukkā was teaching Dhamma, surrounded by a large assembly.
translation_text.sn10.9:1.3: Then a native spirit was so devoted to Sukkā that he went from street to street and from square to square, and on that occasion recited these verses:
translation_text.sn10.9:2.1: “What’s up with these people in Rājagaha?
translation_text.sn10.9:2.2: They sleep like they’ve been drinking mead!
translation_text.sn10.9:2.4: as she’s teaching the state free of death.
translation_text.sn10.9:3.2: it’s as if they drink it up,
translation_text.sn10.9:3.4: like travelers enjoying a cool cloud.”
